# Error-Handling Checklist: Command, workflow, manifest, and file-layout reference

**Purpose**: Validate that DOC-007 requirements and planning artifacts define generator failure modes, stale-output check behavior, source/parsing/internal exits, diagnostics, read-only check behavior, and recovery guidance before implementation.
**Created**: 2026-06-17
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are generator source, parsing, output-write, and internal failure classes classified separately from stale generated output? [Resolved, Spec FR-028, Plan Generator Error Handling Contract, Contract Diagnostics]
- [x] CHK002 Are absent optional repository surfaces defined as non-errors that are labeled absent or omitted without invented facts? [Completeness, Spec Edge Cases, Data Model PlatformMapping]
- [x] CHK003 Are generation-mode safeguards defined so source/parsing/internal errors cannot publish unsupported or partially trusted reference rows? [Resolved, Spec FR-029, Plan Generator Error Handling Contract]
- [x] CHK004 Are malformed JSON, malformed or missing frontmatter, and missing required metadata fields covered as parsing failures with an exit-code contract? [Resolved, Spec Edge Cases, Spec FR-028, Contract Diagnostics]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK005 Is stale generated output unambiguously defined as exit `1`, distinct from source/parsing/internal errors that exit `2`? [Clarity, Spec FR-013, Contract Check]
- [x] CHK006 Are exit-`2` diagnostics required to include an error category, repo-relative source path when available, concise cause, and stderr routing? [Resolved, Spec FR-030, Contract Diagnostics]
- [x] CHK007 Are stale-output diagnostics required to list stale generated page paths and the exact `pnpm --dir docs-site reference:generate` recovery command without implying source corruption? [Clarity, Contract Check, Quickstart Check Reference Freshness]
- [x] CHK008 Is check mode specified as fully read-only, including no create, rewrite, delete, format, package/config update, or existing-doc link mutation in exit `1` or exit `2` paths? [Resolved, Spec FR-013, Plan Generator Error Handling Contract, Contract Check]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK009 Do exit codes align across spec, plan, contract, quickstart, and docs-site `validate` handoff? [Consistency, Spec FR-013, FR-021, Contract Commands, Quickstart Validate Docs Site]
- [x] CHK010 Do source allowlist exclusions align with failure behavior for `.git`, repo-relative `.worktrees`, `node_modules`, generated reference output, network sources, user-local installs, and pasted JSON? [Consistency, Spec FR-014, Plan Source Allowlist And Evidence Boundary]
- [x] CHK011 Are optional surface absence rules consistent with the requirement that source facts must come only from checked-in repository files? [Consistency, Spec FR-004, Spec Edge Cases, Data Model PlatformMapping]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK012 Can reviewers objectively verify that check mode leaves the working tree unchanged for stale output and source/parsing/internal errors? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec SC-005]
- [x] CHK013 Can reviewers objectively verify that stale generated output reports actionable page paths and a recovery command? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec SC-004, Contract Check]
- [x] CHK014 Can reviewers objectively verify that exit-`2` source/parsing/internal failures are actionable when the error has no single source path? [Resolved, Spec FR-030, Spec SC-010, Contract Diagnostics]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK015 Are missing required source files, unreadable files, and directory-read edge cases covered separately from absent optional surfaces? [Coverage, Spec Edge Cases, Plan Source Allowlist And Evidence Boundary]
- [x] CHK016 Are read failures from allowlisted checked-in source paths required to name the affected path rather than falling back to a generic failure? [Coverage, Contract Check]
- [x] CHK017 Is recovery guidance defined for exit `1` stale output, exit `2` source/parsing errors, and exit `2` internal errors without expanding into DOC-008/DOC-010 troubleshooting depth? [Resolved, Spec Validation And Handoff Boundaries, Plan Generator Error Handling Contract, Spec FR-018]
- [x] CHK018 Are generator failure-mode requirements scoped to local docs-site generation without changing plugin behavior, manifests, generated payloads, install flow, hooks, marketplace behavior, release automation, or GitHub Actions? [Scope, Spec FR-015, FR-022]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK019 Are source-path normalization failures required to report repo-relative path evidence while allowing a checkout under a parent `.worktrees/` directory? [Edge Cases, Spec FR-014, Plan Source Allowlist And Evidence Boundary]
- [x] CHK020 Are generated output write failures in generate mode classified without weakening the read-only guarantees of check mode? [Resolved, Spec FR-029, Plan Generator Error Handling Contract, Contract Diagnostics]
- [x] CHK021 Are malformed manifest JSON and malformed Markdown/frontmatter metadata failures required to avoid publishing unsupported source facts or inferred notes? [Resolved, Spec FR-028, FR-029, Contract Diagnostics]

## Remediation Notes

- Initial pass found nine error-handling gaps around generator failure classification, generation-mode safeguards, malformed JSON/frontmatter handling, exit-`2` diagnostics, read-only check behavior, pathless internal diagnostics, bounded recovery guidance, output-write failures, and unsupported-row prevention.
- Gaps were remediated by adding `spec.md` FR-028 through FR-030 and SC-010, adding a `plan.md` generator error-handling contract, aligning `contracts/reference-generator.md` diagnostics, and expanding `data-model.md` `FreshnessCheck` fields.

## Verification Rerun - 2026-06-17

- [x] CHK022 Does the updated spec and plan now classify stale output separately from source, parsing, output-write, and internal failures? [Resolved Verification, Spec FR-028, Plan Generator Error Handling Contract]
- [x] CHK023 Does generate mode now require in-memory source collection, parsing, validation, and rendering before generated page writes? [Resolved Verification, Spec FR-029, Plan Generator Error Handling Contract]
- [x] CHK024 Are malformed JSON, malformed or missing frontmatter, and missing required metadata fields now parsing failures with deterministic exit-`2` behavior? [Resolved Verification, Spec FR-028, Contract Diagnostics]
- [x] CHK025 Are exit-`2` diagnostics now required to use stderr with category, concise cause, source/output path, or phase? [Resolved Verification, Spec FR-030, Contract Diagnostics]
- [x] CHK026 Is check mode now fully read-only for generated files, docs-site package/config files, sidebar config, and existing docs links across exit `1` and exit `2`? [Resolved Verification, Spec FR-013, Plan Generator Error Handling Contract]
- [x] CHK027 Can pathless internal failures now be diagnosed by generator phase and bounded recovery action? [Resolved Verification, Spec FR-030, Data Model FreshnessCheck]
- [x] CHK028 Is stale-output recovery now bounded to running `pnpm --dir docs-site reference:generate`, reviewing generated Markdown diff, and rerunning check? [Resolved Verification, Plan Generator Error Handling Contract]
- [x] CHK029 Are generate-mode output-write failures classified without weakening check-mode immutability? [Resolved Verification, Spec FR-029, Contract Diagnostics]
- [x] CHK030 Are unsupported source facts and inferred notes prevented when source/parsing/internal failures occur? [Resolved Verification, Spec FR-029, Contract Content Contract]
